item.title: 🇱🇧 Hezbollah Rejects Lebanon Ceasefire Agreement
item.description: Hezbollah leader rejected the latest Israel-Lebanon ceasefire as "disgraceful" and called for Israel to withdraw from Lebanon. The rejection was coordinated with Iran.

item.title: ⚔️ UNIFIL Peacekeeper Killed in Lebanon After Attack
item.description: A UN peacekeeper was killed after a UNIFIL position in southern Lebanon was attacked amid ongoing ceasefire negotiations between Israel and Hezbollah.

item.title: 🇵🇸 Gaza Death Toll Since Ceasefire Nears 1,000
item.description: 11 more Palestinians were killed in Gaza strikes in 24 hours as the death toll since the ceasefire began approaches 1,000. Israel continues to deepen its occupation.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Bank of Japan Expected to Raise Rates in June
item.description: The BOJ is expected to raise interest rates this month unless a sharp escalation in the Middle East conflict upends markets. Rising fuel costs from the energy shock are adding to inflation pressure.

item.title: 🇸🇪 Sweden Seizes Ship Suspected of Illegally Exporting Ukrainian Grain
item.description: Sweden seized a vessel suspected of illegally exporting Ukrainian grain from Russian-occupied territories. This is the first time a foreign court has approved such a seizure following a Ukrainian request.

item.title: 🇮🇩 Indonesia arrests deputy minister in major anti-corruption sweep
item.description: Deputy Minister for Immigration Silmy Karim was arrested after 10 hours of questioning, one of several high-profile corruption cases this week including the former head of the president's free meals program.

item.title: 🇸🇴 Heavy gunfire in Mogadishu as Somalia election dispute escalates
item.description: Somali troops and opposition-allied militias exchanged fire in the capital after the president extended his term by a year, with opposition calling for protests and civilians fleeing the city.

item.title: 🏔️ Missing Everest guide found alive after six days
item.description: Dawa Sherpa, who went missing near the summit on May 29, was spotted crawling toward base camp by cleanup crews in what officials are calling a miracle survival.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Ex-Merkel Advisor Links German UN Security Council Loss to Israel Stance
item.description: A former advisor to Angela Merkel said Germany's steadfast support for Israel may have contributed to its failed bid for a UN Security Council seat, citing accusations of double standards from the international community.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Council Chief: Western Balkans Enlargement Is Key Geopolitical Investment
item.description: European Council President Antonio Costa called EU enlargement to the Western Balkans the bloc's most important geopolitical investment, signaling continued commitment to regional integration despite ongoing challenges.

item.title: 🇺🇦 All EU States Back Formal Accession Talks With Ukraine After Hungary Deal
item.description: All 27 EU member states approved opening the first cluster of accession negotiations with Ukraine following an agreement with Hungary on minority rights protections, clearing a major hurdle in Kyiv's path to EU membership.

item.title: 🇬🇱 Rubio: Greenland Is Part of Denmark "For Now"
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio's remarks about Greenland suggest Washington has not abandoned interest in the Arctic island despite Danish objections, raising new questions about US intentions.

item.title: 🇧🇳 Brunei Sultan appoints sons as ministers in major cabinet reshuffle
item.description: Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah announced a significant cabinet shake-up, creating new portfolios and appointing two of his younger sons as ministers in a move seen as preparing for succession.

item.title: 🕵️ Five Eyes alliance warns China targeting personnel via fake job ads
item.description: China is targeting Five Eyes intelligence alliance personnel through fake job postings on LinkedIn, Indeed and Upwork to access classified information. Chinese agents pose as HR consultants and pressure applicants to provide sensitive data.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇳🇿 China Bans New Zealand Lawmakers Over Taiwan Visit
item.description: China barred four New Zealand MPs from entering mainland China, Hong Kong, and Macau for one year after they visited Taiwan. Wellington said it would express concerns to Beijing.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany Loses UN Security Council Seat to Austria and Portugal
item.description: Germany failed to secure a temporary UN Security Council seat, with its support for Ukraine and close ties to Israel potentially costing Berlin the vote. Austria and Portugal won the two available seats.

item.title: 🏛️ US House Votes to Limit Trump's Iran War Powers
item.description: The House passed a war powers resolution 215-208, with four Republicans joining Democrats in a rare rebuke of President Trump's handling of the Iran conflict.

item.title: ⚔️ Russia Pummels Ukrainian Cities as Battlefield Advances Stall
item.description: Russia is intensifying air attacks on Ukrainian cities to compensate for a sputtering ground campaign, with advances slowing to a near halt over the past month.

item.title: 🇬🇧🇷🇴 UK Summons Russian Ambassador After Drone Strikes Romania
item.description: Britain summoned Russia's ambassador after a Russian drone struck a residential building in NATO member Romania, calling the attack unacceptable.

item.title: 🇦🇫🇷🇺 Taliban signs weapons deal with Russia, warns Pakistan
item.description: Afghanistan's Taliban regime signed a military-technical cooperation agreement with Russia on May 27, focusing on repairing Russian-made equipment. Taliban defense minister warned Pakistan would "soon no longer dare" to attack Afghan territory.

item.title: 🇦🇪💻 UAE sees opportunity in Hormuz crisis to protect global data
item.description: As Iran threatens subsea cables through the Strait of Hormuz, the UAE is positioning itself to become a critical hub for data security. Over 95% of intercontinental data traffic travels through subsea cables.

item.title: 🇯🇵🇨🇳 Can Japan's Takaichi repair fractured China ties?
item.description: Japan-China relations are at their most difficult point in years. Despite PM Takaichi's hawkish reputation, analysts recall how Abe Shinzo—once viewed similarly—ultimately stabilized relations with Beijing.

item.title: ⚔️🇺🇸 US delaying weapons deliveries to allies, raising reliability concerns
item.description: Multiple US allies have been told their weapons deliveries are being slowed as Washington prioritizes replenishing its own stockpiles. The move has sent shockwaves through European and Asian defense communities.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukrainian Drones Strike St. Petersburg Oil Terminal as Zelenskyy Vows Escalation
item.description: Ukrainian long-range drones hit an oil terminal in St. Petersburg, setting it ablaze just days before Putin's economic forum. The attack came as Russia launched its largest drone barrage on Ukraine in months, killing 23 civilians.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iranian Drone Attack on Kuwait Airport Kills One, Tests Fragile Ceasefire
item.description: Iranian drones heavily damaged Kuwait International Airport's passenger terminal, killing an Indian national and wounding 63. Kuwait expelled two Iranian diplomats as the four-month-old US-Iran ceasefire showed increasing strain.
